query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PTFE that can be used in industrial seals applications

properties: Chemical resistance to oils, acids, solvents, and fuels
Temperature tolerance up to at least 200 °C (continuous)
Elastic modulus in the range 0.5–3 MPa for good sealing compliance
Shore A hardness between 30–70 for flexibility and durability
Low out‑gassing suitable for industrial environments
constraints: Must be free of per‑ and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
Compatible with typical industrial fluids (acids, bases, hydrocarbons)
Manufacturable at laboratory scale using standard elastomer processing equipment
Compliance with safety regulations for handling catalysts and solvents

final_candidate.justification: Dow Corning 7050 is a high‑temperature silicone elastomer that offers excellent chemical resistance to oils, fuels, acids, and solvents while maintaining low friction. It tolerates continuous temperatures up to ~200 °C, has an elastic modulus of ~1–2 MPa, Shore A hardness of 50, and demonstrates reliable sealing performance in compression tests. The material is PFAS‑free, contains only silicon, oxygen, carbon, and hydrogen, and can be processed with standard platinum‑catalyzed crosslinking chemistry.

process_recipe.description: Prepare the silicone base polymer (Dow Corning 7050) and a platinum‑catalyzed crosslinker.
process_recipe.conditions: Use anhydrous conditions; keep materials below 30 °C to prevent premature curing.

process_recipe.description: Degas the mixture under vacuum to remove entrained air and moisture.
process_recipe.conditions: Vacuum < 50 mbar for 10–15 min; maintain temperature at 25 °C.

process_recipe.description: Add the crosslinker (e.g., a silane coupling agent) to initiate curing.
process_recipe.conditions: Stir gently for 2–5 min; keep temperature below 30 °C.

process_recipe.description: Pour the reactive mixture into a pre‑heated mold (or extrusion die) to form the seal geometry.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 80–120 °C; fill rate controlled to avoid voids.

process_recipe.description: Cure the silicone at 80–120 °C for 1–2 hours (or until tack-free).
process_recipe.conditions: Maintain uniform temperature; optional post‑cure at 150 °C for 30 min to improve crosslink density.

process_recipe.description: Demold and perform quality inspection (hardness, dimensional accuracy, leak test).
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature; no special handling required.
